title: Coding Prompts, Scoped Edits, and Regression Control · Guided Eval Studio
summary: Implement a repository change with tests and minimal unrelated churn. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a coding-agent implementation brief while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.
Ground implementation in repository commands, architecture, dependency policy, and local instructions.
Specify risk-proportionate tests and observable regression checks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model

workshop case — Use a changed workshop case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-implementation-evidence-gqc61p" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Implementation Evidence into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-implementation-evidence-gqc61p
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Implementation Evidence structural check is only one gate. a coding-agent implementation brief must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.
